tagged_story_for_tts:
"Trump isn’t a self-made billionaire—he’s a trust-fund arsonist.<[silence:400]>

The myth? He built an empire.<[silence:400]> The reality? He inherited $413M (2018 NYT investigation), then burned through it via six corporate bankruptcies, tax dodges, and fraud so blatant that NY courts ruled his net worth was a ‘fantasy’ (2024 $454M judgment).<[silence:400]> His ‘business genius’? Stiffing contractors, laundering cash through condos (per Forbes 2016), and grifting $7M from a kids’ cancer charity (Washington Post, 2019).<[silence:400]>

The paradox? His supporters call him ‘anti-establishment’ while he appointed more Goldman Sachs alumni than Obama and signed a tax cut that handed 83% of benefits to the top 1%.<[silence:400]> His ‘populism’ is a brand—like his steaks, university, and vodka, all failures propped up by suckers.<[silence:400]>

The brutal truth? Trump’s only consistent skill is monetizing rage while outsourcing the fallout.<[silence:400]> You’re not his base.<[silence:400]> You’re his exit strategy.<[silence:400]>

Ask yourself:<[silence:100]> If a con this obvious works on you, what else are you paying to ignore?"
clean_story:
"Trump isn’t a self-made billionaire—he’s a trust-fund arsonist.

The myth? He built an empire. The reality? He inherited $413M (2018 NYT investigation), then burned through it via six corporate bankruptcies, tax dodges, and fraud so blatant that NY courts ruled his net worth was a ‘fantasy’ (2024 $454M judgment). His ‘business genius’? Stiffing contractors, laundering cash through condos (per Forbes 2016), and grifting $7M from a kids’ cancer charity (Washington Post, 2019).

The paradox? His supporters call him ‘anti-establishment’ while he appointed more Goldman Sachs alumni than Obama and signed a tax cut that handed 83% of benefits to the top 1%. His ‘populism’ is a brand—like his steaks, university, and vodka, all failures propped up by suckers.

The brutal truth? Trump’s only consistent skill is monetizing rage while outsourcing the fallout. You’re not his base. You’re his exit strategy.

Ask yourself: If a con this obvious works on you, what else are you paying to ignore?"
